entities.name: HOT WATER EXTRACTABLE CARBON
entities.description: The fraction of soil organic carbon that is soluble in hot water, serving as an indicator for labile carbon and soil health

entities.name: SOIL ORGANIC CARBON
entities.description: The carbon content found within the soil

entities.name: HOT WATER
entities.description: The medium used to extract the soluble carbon fraction from the soil

entities.name: LABILE CARBON
entities.description: The easily decomposable portion of organic matter that can be readily broken down by microorganisms

entities.name: ORGANIC MATTER
entities.description: The organic components of the soil

entities.name: SOIL MICROORGANISMS
entities.description: Microbes in the soil responsible for decomposing labile carbon

entities.name: NUTRIENT CYCLING
entities.description: The process of nutrient movement and exchange in the soil

entities.name: MICROBIAL ACTIVITY
entities.description: The biological processes and functions performed by soil microorganisms

entities.name: SOIL HEALTH
entities.description: The capacity of the soil to function as a vital living ecosystem

entities.name: SOIL QUALITY
entities.description: The capacity of the soil to function within given limits

entities.name: MANAGEMENT PRACTICES
entities.description: Agricultural or land management interventions that affect soil carbon content
